title: ranunculus bulbosus subsp. dissectus
intro: Ranunculus bulbosus subsp. dissectus, also known as the cleft buttercup, is a subspecies of the Ranunculus bulbosus plant, a type of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Ranunculus bulbosus subsp. dissectus include its ability to treat conditions such as arthritis, gout, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. The plant is typically prepared as a tincture or infusion, and may also be used in salves and creams to take advantage of its antiseptic properties.
